calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn; Req. For Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Jul-18-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James Farinaro, Esq. of the Law Office of James Farinaro, Tel. 510.553.1200, for petitioner and moving party, via Court Call;
John T. McGlothin, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2224, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. PETITIONER ZACHARY LUKE'S Petition For Writ Of Mandamus is denied. Petitioner has not established that the administrative decision is contrary to the weight of the evidence. The evidence supports the hearing officer's conclusion that the incorrect time of arrest was a clerical error and that Officer Woods' reports are trustworthy. (Administrative Record ("AR") CT000008-CT000009, DS-367; CT 000015-CT000017, Incident Report; CT000047-CT000048, Hearing transcript, pp. 10:22-11:4.) The evidence supports the hearing officer's conclusion that Officer Woods had reasonable cause to believe Petitioner was driving a motor vehicle while under the influence of alcohol, and therefore, probable cause to arrest Petitioner. Upon making contact with Petitioner, Officer Woods smelled alcohol and noticed Petitioner had watery, bloodshot eyes. (AR CT000008, DS-367; CT000015, Incident Report.) Petitioner was unable to properly perform the field sobriety tests and failed the Preliminary Alcohol Screening tests. (AR CT000016-CT000017, Incident Report.) The evidence supports the hearing officer's conclusion that Petitioner was properly admonished. In an administrative proceeding the determination of credibility is within the province of the hearing officer. (Mullin v. Dept. of Real Estate (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 295, 300-01.) Petitioner has not established that the hearing officer's subjective interpretation of the evidence was incorrect. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Ntc Of Mtn; Req. For Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Continued To Jul-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER ZACHARY LUKE's Petition For Writ Of Mandamus is continued to July 18, 2014 to give the parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The parties must deliver courtesy copies of the Petition for Writ of Mandate, Opposition filed March 21, 2014 and the administrative record to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 30, 2014 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the Notice Motion, filed on May 29, 2014, and Opposition, filed on June 5, 2014. Judge: John K. Stewart, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/JKS
